content:
In this practice lab, I installed Apache2 and deployed a site that is different from the default. 9 min read Just now -- Apache2 logoIntroduction Apache2 is the most widely used, free, and open-source web server software. It securely processes incoming HTTP/HTTPS requests from web browsers and deliver websites or dynamic web applications to the user. For this lab, an already developed website has been made available. It is my duty to configure the website, deploy it and make it available to the public through Apache2. Objectives • Confirm the content of my mock website • Install Apache2 • Configure the website • Enable additional features on the website Check content of my website The mock website was saved inside the /opt/devel/ourcompany directory. To take a look at the contents, I entered the command: ls -l /opt/devel/ourcompanyAs seen below, there’s a bunch of HTML pages and a CSS stylesheet that form the website. total 20 -rwxr-xr-x 1 student student 561 Jun 19 13:27 aboutus.html -rwxr-xr-x 1 student student 551 Jun 19 13:27 contact.html -rwxr-xr-x 1 student student 95 Jun 19 13:27 footer.html -rwxr-xr-x 1 student student 596 Jun 19 13:27 index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 student student 777 Jun 19 13:27 style.cssInstall Apache2 At this time, my machine did not have any web server running. I fixed that by updating the list of packages and then installing the Apache2 package. I applied the following commands: sudo apt update sudo apt install apache2The dependencies of the Apache2 package was displayed on my screen. After that, I clicked y at the prompt to confirm the installation. Reading package lists... Done Building dependency tree Reading state information... Done The following additional packages will be installed: apache2-bin apache2-data apache2-utils libapr1 libaprutil1 libaprutil1-dbd-sqlite3 libaprutil1-ldap libbrotli1 libjansson4 liblua5.2-0 ssl-cert Suggested packages: apache2-doc apache2-suexec-pristine | apache2-suexec-custom www-browser openssl-blacklist The following NEW packages will be installed: apache2 apache2-bin apache2-data apache2-utils libapr1 libaprutil1 libaprutil1-dbd-sqlite3 libaprutil1-ldap libbrotli1 libjansson4 liblua5.2-0 ssl-cert 0 upgraded, 12 newly installed, 0 to remove and 23 not upgraded. Need to get 2628 kB of archives. After this operation, 8946 kB of additional disk space will be used. Do you want to continue? [Y/n] yAs soon as Apache2 was installed, I confirmed whether it was running using the command below: sudo service apache2 statusThe output simply stated that apache2 is not running. To start the Apache web service I used the following command: sudo service apache2 startWith that, Apache2 started serving its default webpage through my machine’s external IP address. To confirm this, I entered the External IP address in a new browser tab. Press enter or click to view image in full size Apache2 Debian Default PageThis page confirms that the Apache2 web server is working and also gives instructions on how to configure Apache2. Configure site Now, I need to ensure Apache2 serves my mock site and not the default page. In the Linux file system, the list of available sites configuration files is located in /etc/apache2/sites-available. I took a look at the content: ls -l /etc/apache2/sites-availableThis displayed two website configuration files. The default website configured by 000-default.conf and the default encrypted website is managed by default-ssl.conf. total 12 -rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1332 May 3 22:3 000-default.conf -rw-r--r-- 1 root root 6338 May 3 22:3 default-ssl.confI took a look at 000-default.conf to learn more about how the default website is configured. cat /etc/apache2/sites-available/000-default.confWhile studying the configuration file, I learnt that the lines that begin with # are comments that give information on the different parameters and settings. # The ServerName directive sets the request scheme, hostname and port that # the server uses to identify itself. This is used when creating # redirection URLs. In the context of virtual hosts, the ServerName # specifies what hostname must appear in the request's Host: header to # match this virtual host. For the default virtual host (this file) this # value is not decisive as it is used as a last resort host regardless. # However, you must set it for any further virtual host explicitly. #ServerName www.example.com ServerAdmin webmaster@localhost DocumentRoot /var/www/html # Available loglevels: trace8, ..., trace1, debug, info, notice, warn, # error, crit, alert, emerg. # It is also possible to configure the loglevel for particular # modules, e.g. #LogLevel info ssl:warn ErrorLog ${APACHE_LOG_DIR}/error.log CustomLog ${APACHE_LOG_DIR}/access.log combined # For most configuration files from conf-available/, which are # enabled or disabled at a global level, it is possible to # include a line for only one particular virtual host. For example the # following line enables the CGI configuration for this host only # after it has been globally disabled with "a2disconf". #Include conf-available/serve-cgi-bin.conf # vim: syntax=apache ts=4 sw=4 sts=4 sr noet The actual configuration is just six lines of the file: ServerAdmin webmaster@localhost DocumentRoot /var/www/html ErrorLog ${APACHE_LOG_DIR}/error.log CustomLog ${APACHE_LOG_DIR}/access.log combined It shows that the service was configured to listen on port 80 for all IPs. The next lines state the email address for the administrator of the service, the main path for the website, and the paths for the error and access log files. Now I can start the process of replacing the default site with my mock website It’s standard practice to have all websites inside /var/www. I applied the command below to move my mock website from /opt/devel/ourcompany into /var/www/ourcompany. sudo mv /opt/devel/ourcompany /var/www/ourcompanyIn order to see if it worked, I confirmed the contents of /var/www. ls -l /var/wwwThe output shows that my mock website named ourcompany is now in the right location. total 8 drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 Jun 19 13:28 html drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 Jun 19 13:27 ourcompanyNext, I created my own website configuration. To do that, I moved to the /etc/apache2/sites-available directory. Then, I copied the default site configuration and edited the new configuration file to suit my mock site. I applied the following commands: cd /etc/apache2/sites-available sudo cp 000-default.conf 001-ourcompany.conf sudo nano 001-ourcompany.confThe last command opened the nano text editor. In the editor, I changed the DocumentRoot (directory where website files are stored) from /var/www/html to /var/www/ourcompany. The first path belongs to the default website and had to be changed to my mock site’s location. # The ServerName directive sets the request scheme, hostname and port that # the server uses to identify itself. This is used when creating # redirection URLs. In the context of virtual hosts, the ServerName # specifies what hostname must appear in the request's Host: header to # match this virtual host. For the default virtual host (this file) this # value is not decisive as it is used as a last resort host regardless. # However, you must set it for any further virtual host explicitly. #ServerName www.example.com ServerAdmin webmaster@localhost DocumentRoot /var/www/ourcompany # Available loglevels: trace8, ..., trace1, debug, info, notice, warn, # error, crit, alert, emerg. # It is also possible to configure the loglevel for particular # modules, e.g. #LogLevel info ssl:warn ErrorLog ${APACHE_LOG_DIR}/error.log CustomLog ${APACHE_LOG_DIR}/access.log combined # For most configuration files from conf-available/, which are # enabled or disabled at a global level, it is possible to # include a line for only one particular virtual host. For example the # following line enables the CGI configuration for this host only # after it has been globally disabled with "a2disconf". #Include conf-available/serve-cgi-bin.conf # vim: syntax=apache ts=4 sw=4 sts=4 sr noet After editing, I exited the editor, confirmed the changes and clicked Enter to return to the command line. Now, my mock site has been configured but not enabled. On Apache2, sites that are available are not necessarily enabled. To enable my site, I entered the command: sudo a2ensite 001-ourcompany.confThe output shows that I have to reload Apache2 to activate this change. Enabling site 001-ourcompany. To activate the new configuration, you need to run: service apache2 reloadTo disable the default site, I applied the following command: sudo a2dissite 000-default.confThis will be fully applied when Apache2 is reloaded. Site 000-default disabled. To activate the new configuration, you need to run: service apache2 reloadTo confirm this, I took a look at the content of the enabled sites directory: ls -l /etc/apache2/sites-enabledThe output showed that my site is the only one on the enabled list. total 0 lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 38 Aug 11 12:19 001-ourcompany.conf -> ../sites-available/001-ourcompany.confHowever, I must reload Apache2 to activate the new configurations so my site will be served instead of the default site. I applied the command below: sudo service apache2 reloadThe command doesn’t give any output if it succeeds; to know if it worked, I had to visit the webpage. Press enter or click to view image in full size Success! At the bottom of the website, I saw an horizontal line dividing the content from where a footer should be, but the footer isn’t showing anything. To confirm whether a footer exists, I took a look at the index.html file for my mock site using the cat command: cat /var/www/ourcompany/index.htmlThe output shows that a footer exists but it is not enabled. Our Institutional website

Welcome!

This is our institutional website. You can learn more about us orget in contact with us.

The footer uses a feature provided by Apache2, called Server Side Includes. This is linked to a module that needs to be enabled. There’s a long list of available modules on Apache2. In order to know which one to enable, I referred to Apache2 documentation for guidance. I found that the module for Server Side Includes is named include. I ran the following command to enable include: sudo a2enmod includeSince this is a new functionality and not just a configuration, the response required that I restart Apache2. To do that, I entered: sudo service apache2 restartNow that the include function has been enabled for the web server, I have to configure my site to use Server Side Includes, First I opened the configuration file on nano editor: sudo nano /etc/apache2/sites-available/001-ourcompany.confThen I added a snippet after the DocumentRoot line: # The ServerName directive sets the request scheme, hostname and port that # the server uses to identify itself. This is used when creating # redirection URLs. In the context of virtual hosts, the ServerName # specifies what hostname must appear in the request's Host: header to # match this virtual host. For the default virtual host (this file) this # value is not decisive as it is used as a last resort host regardless. # However, you must set it for any further virtual host explicitly. #ServerName www.example.com ServerAdmin webmaster@localhost DocumentRoot /var/www/ourcompany Options +Includes XBitHack on # Available loglevels: trace8, ..., trace1, debug, info, notice, warn, # error, crit, alert, emerg. # It is also possible to configure the loglevel for particular # modules, e.g. #LogLevel info ssl:warn
